scenario: Unknown reporter (Gate 6 → MIS). A clean, confident On-Label contraindication question from a physician who isn't in our HCP registry (newly joined, not yet onboarded). Gate 6 can't resolve her to a system ID → MIS for identity resolution. Clean isolation: everything else passes — confident classification, product in catalog, native text, no AE, in scope.
input_text:
SOURCE CHANNEL: Web Portal
SUBMISSION PORTAL REF: portal-medinfo-secure-1109

MESSAGE BODY:
To the Medical Affairs Desk,

I am an attending cardiologist who recently joined the Pacific Heart and Vascular Institute in Seattle.

Before initiating any new patients, I want to confirm the approved label. Per the official package insert for CholoClear-X, is the product contraindicated in patients with active hepatic disease or unexplained persistent elevations of serum transaminases? Please confirm the labeled contraindications.

Thank you,
Dr. Evelyn Shaw
Pacific Heart and Vascular Institute
Email: e.shaw@pacificheart.org | Phone: 206-555-0191

on_label_record.inquiry_summary_text: Physician asks whether CholoClear-X is contraindicated in patients with active hepatic disease or persistently elevated serum transaminases, per the approved package insert.

failed_gates: Gate 6: HCP link — 'Evelyn Shaw' not in HCP registry

labeling_notes: GATE 6 ISOLATION (clean): the ONLY failing gate is Gate 6 — Dr. Evelyn Shaw is a real physician but not (yet) in the HCP registry, so resolve_hcp_link returns null. Confident On-Label classification (~0.97 → Gate 2 passes; margin ~0.85 → Gate 3 passes), product in catalog (Gate 5), native digital (Gate 1), no AE (Gate 4), in scope (Gate 8). MIS is attributable to the unknown reporter alone.

WHY MIS, NOT AUTO-APPROVE: the system will NOT invent an HCP id or auto-commit under an unverified identity (avoids duplicate/unverified profiles in Vault). It hands the case to MIS to look up the NPI and register her — then future inquiries from her auto-resolve.

DESIGN A/B WITH case_001: same kind of clean On-Label inquiry, but case_001's reporter (Jonathan Vance) IS registered → Auto-Approve, while this reporter is NOT → MIS via Gate 6. The single variable is registry membership.

CONFIDENCE SIGNAL: a canonical single-intent question (contraindication) scores high (~0.97), unlike the ambiguous storage/competitor questions (0.62–0.72) — consistent evidence that the confidence gate behaves sensibly.
